(born 10 December 1979), or simply **Ferreira Pinto**, is a Brazilian [football](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") [winger](/wiki/Winger_(sport) "Winger (sport)") playing for Italian [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D") club [Ponte San Pietro](/wiki/AC_Ponte_San_Pietro_SSD "AC Ponte San Pietro SSD").

**Adriano Ferreira Pinto** (born 10 December 1979), or simply **Ferreira Pinto**, is a Brazilian [football](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") [winger](/wiki/Winger_(sport) "Winger (sport)") playing for Italian [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D") club [Ponte San Pietro](/wiki/AC_Ponte_San_Pietro_SSD "AC Ponte San Pietro SSD").

He played for [São José Esporte Clube](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Jos%C3%A9_Esporte_Clube "São José Esporte Clube") in Brazil before joining [S.S. Lanciano](/wiki/S.S._Lanciano "S.S. Lanciano") in 2002 in Italy's [Serie C1](/wiki/Serie_C1 "Serie C1"). He then moved up to [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B") with [Perugia](/wiki/Perugia_Calcio "Perugia Calcio") and [Cesena](/wiki/A.C._Cesena "A.C. Cesena").

In October 2008, Ferreira Pinto extended his contract with Atalanta to June 2011.
During the last days of winter's market he signed a contract until June with the [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B") team [Varese](/wiki/A.S._Varese_1910 "A.S. Varese 1910").

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ferreira_Pinto_(footballer,_born_1979)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Ferreira Pinto was born in [Quinta do Sol](/wiki/Quinta_do_Sol "Quinta do Sol"), [Paranà](/wiki/Paran%C3%A1_(state) "Paraná (state)"). He grew up in a very poor family and at the age of 15 he lost his father. Until the age of 18 he worked as bricklayer and day laborer.

In Italy, he met Marianna, whom he married on 7 June 2009 in [Lanciano](/wiki/Lanciano "Lanciano"), [Abruzzo](/wiki/Abruzzo "Abruzzo"). They have a son, José Carlos.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ferreira_Pinto_(footballer,_born_1979)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Ferreira Pinto began his career at [União São João](/wiki/Uni%C3%A3o_S%C3%A3o_Jo%C3%A3o_Esporte_Clube "União São João Esporte Clube") in the [Campeonato Brasileiro Série C](/wiki/Campeonato_Brasileiro_S%C3%A9rie_C "Campeonato Brasileiro Série C"). During his first season he managed 28 goals.

In 2001, he was close to joining [Standard Liège](/wiki/Standard_Li%C3%A8ge "Standard Liège"), but last moment he changed his mind and signed for [Lanciano](/wiki/S.S._Virtus_Lanciano_1924 "S.S. Virtus Lanciano 1924") in [Serie C1](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Prima_Divisione "Lega Pro Prima Divisione"). He played for the *Frentani* until August 2004, scoring 15 goals in 71 matches.

On July 1 2004, he signed for [Perugia](/wiki/A.C._Perugia_Calcio "A.C. Perugia Calcio"), in italian [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B"). He made his debut in the 2–1 win against [Crotone](/wiki/F.C._Crotone "F.C. Crotone") on 11 September 2004. On 21 September, Ferreira Pinto scored his first goal for Perugia in a 2–0 victory against [Treviso](/wiki/Treviso_F.C._1993 "Treviso F.C. 1993").

At the end of the season Perugia failed and Ferreira Pinto signed for [Cesena](/wiki/A.C._Cesena "A.C. Cesena"). He finished 2005–06 season scoring 11 goals in 39 matches.
